demographics.audit_notes: Region 'Great Hills (Tract 342.0)' is a non-standard tract ID. Audit assumes it represents an aggregate of tracts 17.15, 17.16, and 17.17. Values are consistent with this aggregate area for the 1990 Decennial Census.
All values are interpolated between the 1990 and 2000 decennial censuses. Trends appear plausible for the area. Region assumed to be aggregate of tracts 17.15, 17.16, 17.17.
Values align well with the 2000 Decennial Census for the aggregated tracts (17.15, 17.16, 17.17).
Values are interpolated between 2000 Census and 2010 ACS data. This is the first year where ACS data becomes available, improving estimate quality.
Values align well with the 2010 Decennial Census and 2006-2010 ACS 5-Year estimates for the aggregated tracts.
Values are consistent with ACS 5-Year 2011-2015 estimates for the aggregated tracts.
Population value is slightly lower than the 2020 Census aggregate for tracts 17.15, 17.16, 17.17 (~15,100), but within a plausible range. Other values align well with Census/ACS data.
Values are consistent with ACS 5-Year 2017-2021 estimates for the aggregated tracts.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-Year 2018-2022 estimates for the aggregated tracts.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of trends from the 2018-2022 ACS data.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of trends from the 2018-2022 ACS data.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of trends from the 2018-2022 ACS data.

property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census data for the area. New construction permits are a rough estimate based on housing unit growth over the following 5 years.
Values are interpolated. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 5-year period.
Values align with 2000 Census data. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 1995-2000 period.
Values are interpolated. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 2000-2005 period.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 2005-2010 period.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 2010-2015 period.

property.audit_notes: Values align with ACS 2016-2020 and Zillow data. YoY home value change is an annualized average over the 2015-2020 period.

property.audit_notes: The 20% jump in median home value reflects the real estate market boom in Austin during this period.

property.audit_notes: Home value shows a slight decrease, reflecting the market correction in Austin during 2023. Other values are projected.
Projected values based on market stabilization trends observed in late 2023 and early 2024.
Projected values assuming a return to moderate, steady growth in the housing market.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Original `unemployment_rate_pct` renamed to `unemployment_rate`. Values are consistent with 1990 Census for an affluent suburban area. Eviction data unavailable for this period.
All values are interpolated and reflect the strong economic conditions of the mid-1990s tech boom.
Values are consistent with 2000 Census data, reflecting the peak of the dot-com bubble.
Interpolated values reflecting post-recession recovery period. First year ACS data is available for bracketing.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data, reflecting the impact of the Great Recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data, showing strong economic recovery.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 data. Unemployment rate reflects the initial economic shock of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Values align with ACS 2017-2021 data, showing rapid economic recovery post-2020.
Values align with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data.
Projected values based on trends from 2022 ACS and regional economic data.
Projected values based on trends from 2022 ACS and regional economic data.
Projected values based on trends from 2022 ACS and regional economic data.
